title: The Great Debate: Public Transportation vs Private Cars
passage:
The Great Debate: Public Transportation vs Private Cars

In modern cities, the question of how to get around is becoming increasingly significant. Public transportation and private cars are two main options available to commuters. Each has its own advantages and disadvantages. Public transportation includes buses, trains, and subways, which can be cost-effective and reduce traffic congestion. For example, a report from the city council indicated that using buses can save an average commuter about 30% on travel costs compared to owning a car.

On the other hand, private cars offer convenience and flexibility. Drivers can choose their routes and travel times, which can be particularly advantageous for those with busy schedules. However, the increasing number of private vehicles has led to more traffic jams and higher pollution levels in many urban areas. Many experts argue that switching to public transportation could significantly help reduce the carbon footprint of cities.

Moreover, public transportation systems are continually improving. Many cities have invested in high-speed trains and efficient bus routes to make commuting easier and faster. Yet, there are still concerns about the reliability and coverage of public transport, especially in suburban areas where services may be limited. Consequently, some people feel that private cars are still the best option for them.

Ultimately, the choice between public transportation and private cars depends on individual needs and circumstances. While public transport is generally more environmentally friendly, private cars may provide the comfort and convenience that some commuters require. Both methods of transportation have their roles to play in the future of urban mobility.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What is one advantage of public transportation mentioned in the passage?

options.A: It can save money on travel costs.
options.C: It is always faster than driving.
options.D: It is available in all suburban areas.

questions.explanation: The passage states that using buses can save an average commuter about 30% on travel costs, highlighting the financial advantage of public transportation.

questions.text: Public transportation systems are not improving in modern cities.
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that many cities have invested in improving public transportation systems, indicating that they are indeed improving.

questions.text: The author believes that private cars are better than public transportation.
questions.explanation: The author presents both sides but does not state a preference for private cars over public transportation.

questions.text: Match the following benefits with their corresponding transportation type.
Using buses can save an average commuter about __ on travel costs.
questions.explanation: The passage provides the information that using buses can save about 30% on travel costs.

vocabulary.meaning: people who travel regularly to a place, especially to work.
the state of being overcrowded, especially with traffic.
the amount of carbon dioxide emissions for which an individual or organization is responsible.

tactics:
Hướng dẫn

When reading the passage, focus on the main ideas presented in each paragraph. Take notes on advantages and disadvantages of both public transportation and private cars, as these will help you answer the questions effectively. Pay attention to any specific data mentioned, as they can be useful for multiple-choice and sentence completion questions.
